observations.recommendation:
Management Comments:
The management discussed the findings with the three concerned officer and resolve to comply with the audit recommendations. The Municipal Treasurer committed to distribute notice of tax delinquency by the second quarter of 2023 and will take extra efforts to increase collections.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor requires the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit all the prior period unremitted balances to the concerned government agencies. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant confers with the concerned agencies to determine remedies that shall ensure that these long-outstanding balances be validated upon remittance/payment.

Management Comment:
The Municipal Accountant committed to undertake the audit recommendation.

We recommended that management strictly adhere to the provisions of keeping the accounts of the agency and shall specifically undertake the following:
Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to have a regular monthly reconciliation of balances of undeposited collections of the General Fund and Trust Fund to bring the Cash-Local Treasury balances accurate and reliable and fairly presented in the financial statements as of a given period; and
Require/instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile balances in their respective records at least quarterly as mandated by the above-stated Circular to have an accurate and reliable balance of the Petty Cash and Advances for Payroll Fund Account in the financial statements as of a given period.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant resolves the variance with the creditor for a proper determination of LGU’s obligation and check if there were additional charges of interest expense and gross receipt taxes. Further, conduct a regular reconciliation of balances with the creditor.

However, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
the Accounting Office and the General Services Office to render a monthly report on the receipt, issuance, and balances of vaccines and other inventories directly received from outside sources; and
the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the MHO to determine and recognize in the books the full cost of the ending inventory of vaccines as at year-end and to henceforth record future receipts and issuances of the same.

Management Comments:
The management are appreciating the audit team for acknowledging the LGU’s efforts. The management committed to continue their efforts, likewise, to prepare and submit the needed Utilization Report accurately and on time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Sangguniang Bayan use their savings in accordance with Section 336 of RA No. 7160. We further recommended that they prioritize the appropriation of the savings of Personnel Services to the underpaid salaries, retirement, and health benefits of casual personnel and then, other authorized and allowed benefits for all personnel and officials, subject to the PS Limitation.

We recommended that the Municipality conduct meticulous and judicious procurement planning, and resort to the procurement of goods through competitive bidding pursuant to Sections 7 and 10 of the Revised IRR of R.A No. 9184. The Municipality, however, may resort to alternative methods of procurement whenever justified by the conditions provided under Rule XVI of the same IRR. Further, the management shall refrain from paying procurement of goods through reimbursement basis, instead, payments shall be made by checks drawn in the name of the supplier to withhold the applicable taxes due, in conformity with the existing rules and regulations of the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
The management to submit other supporting documents that can justify and establish the reasonableness of these noted expenses for further consideration in audit.
Establish clear-cut guidelines for the communication expenses claimed by its officials and employees; and/or consider adopting the post-paid plans offered by the network providers for more economical reasons and to foster equity in the grant thereof in relation to actual communication needs of the LGU officials.
That if cell cards/e-load are availed, consider purchasing in bulk, following the required procurement procedures and rules on withholding taxes, distribute the same in accordance with the guidelines and surrender used/consumed cellphone load cards.

We recommended that the Management submit supporting documents to prove that the electricity consumption of the subject buildings or facilities is used for public purposes to substantiate the electricity expense amounting to ₱3,263,223.07.

We recommended the Management to submit the basis or any exemption to the law and/or concurrence of COMELEC for further consideration in audit for the appointment of sectoral representative and on the eligibility/qualifications of the newly appointed sectoral representative and the Management to comply with Sections 41, 45 and 446 of RA No. 7160, otherwise stop the payment of salaries and other personnel benefits to these officials.

We recommended that the Management:
Strictly observe that payment/ disbursements were properly supported with complete documentation as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012 dated June 14, 2012.
Prepare Pre-Inspection Report prior to the conduct of repair and maintenance to ascertain the necessity and the scope of the work to be done as well as to update/prepare repair history of the service vehicle and Post-Inspection Report after the scope of work has been accomplished.
Require the authorized inspector and witness to certify the mode and manner of disposition of waste material.
Submit justification on the need to replace/ repair for Toyota Innova with Plate Number SAA 6323 under Check Number 17978 dated March 14, 2022, amounting to P109,310.0,0 and determine if should there be any liability of the accountable officer in charge on the proper safekeeping of the aforementioned service vehicle and take necessary action, as circumstances warrant.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Impose the required expanded withholding tax on the payments to the professionals, consultants, other recipients of talent fees, rental of personal properties and suppliers of services. Except, when the annual income is below the threshold of P250,000.00 and upon submission of Income Payee’s Sworn Declaration of Gross Receipts/Sales; and
Impose the 20% final withholding tax on winnings of individuals above P10,000.00;
To pay the winners of contested activities individually for the proper evaluation of imposing the final withholding tax; and
Adhere to the above provisions, rules, and regulations.

Management Comments:
The Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO committed to undertake the audit recommendation.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the salaries and wages of all salaried and qualified personnel of the LGU are paid in accordance with the four tranches of the Modified Salary Schedule and Salary Standardization Law or RA No. 11466, subject again to compliance with the PS limitation.
In case funds are insufficient, it is reiterated that the Sangguniang Bayan formulate a modified salary schedule adopting authorized salary rates at a uniform percentage across all government positions covered by RA No. 11466.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to construct a contract that states specific qualifications, tasks, duties, responsibilities, work schedule and time, and other roles and functions related to their job. We further recommended that they ensure the contract is perfected between the LGU and the service provider, where both parties will agree between the terms and conditions by affixing their signatures.

We recommended that the Management:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to refrain paying regular and recurring expenses out of the petty cash fund such as salary, reimbursable Representation and Travelling Allowance (RATA), traveling expenses, repairs and maintenance expense, overtime pay, office supplies, and the like.
Limit the payment out of the petty cash fund to petty expenses, which could not be conveniently paid thru check, not exceeding P1,000.00 as prescribed under the Manual on New Government Accounting for Local Government Units.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary reclassification of all costs to Merchandise Inventory account considering there is a clear intention of the management to sell the lots in the LGU Village per Municipal Ordinance No. 2017-012 February 20, 2017. The Municipal Accountant should also ensure that the provisions and policies under IPSAS Nos. 12 and 17 are consistently complied.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant already reclassified the costs incurred for LGU Village into Merchandise Inventory.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management should only allow payments of prior period expenses if strictly compliant with RA 7160, otherwise, the absence of appropriation especially those with budgetary requirements may cause the annual budget being inoperative in part or in its entirety. 

We also recommended that the management strictly comply with the following:

a) Lawful expenditures and obligations incurred during a fiscal year should be taken up in the accounts of same year;

b) Payments of prior years’ expenses should not be charged against the current appropriation unless specifically allowed by laws, rules and regulations; and

c) All expenses are properly obligated and accounted in the proper accounting period.

5.20. We also recommended that the Management adhere strictly with the budgetary and funding requirements for LDRRMF as provided in RA 10121, RA 7160 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the salaries and wages of all salaried and qualified personnel of the LGU are paid in accordance with the four tranches of the Modified Salary Schedule and Salary Standardization Law or RA No. 11466, subject to compliance with the PS limitation.

In case that funds are insufficient, it was recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan formulate a modified salary schedule adopting authorized salary rates at a uniform percentage across all government positions covered by RA 11466.

a) Stop charging ineligible expenses against the 20% Development Fund; and

b) Ensure that the appropriation of project expenditures and/or utilization of Funds are strictly in accordance with the JMC.
observations.managementAction: The management scrutinized the EDF Plan and Budget and assured the compliance with the law, rules and regulations. Any continuing appropriations that are MOOE in nature will be realigned to other programs this July, 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to 1) show for verification in audit the land titles as proof of ownership of the parcels of land, otherwise, the LGU’s rights and ownership over the lots are doubtful and may be exposed to future adverse claims; 2) provide for details and other more reliable documents supporting the increase in the land account and 3) ensure that legal rights and possession over the lots are fully established before recognition in the books of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit the above-listed documents for all the projects that were implemented in CY 2019 and those which construction or implementation are still on-going in order that an objective evaluation of the contracts and proper inspection be undertaken.
observations.managementAction: The management already attaching the required documents in all transactions related to infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management ensure that deliveries of procured goods be inspected and validated by the audit team in compliance with the laws, rules and regulations. It also recommended that the resident auditor be notified within 24 hours from acceptance, the time, day and place of delivery to allow the auditor to confirm the conduct of inspection and validation.
observations.managementAction: The management repeatedly reminding the GSO to present every deliveries of items purchased by the LGU.
The management already complying with the laws, rules and regulations in relation to the utilization of DRRM fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:

a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the transactions to the proper asset accounts and the Supply Officer to establish accountability over these assets; and

b) Ensure compliance with IPSAS and applicable accounting rules and regulations on the recording and classification of financial transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant already reclassified the transaction to PPE account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

a) Address and justify the noted inconsistencies in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund out of the Current Year Appropriation and Continuing Appropriations;

b) Stop the charging of expenditures out of the Fund which are not allowed by the reviewing authorities, the law and other rules; and

c) Ensure that the 20% Development Fund is appropriated and utilized in accordance with the law, rules and regulations, otherwise the transactions shall be suspended or disallowed, as the case may be.
observations.managementAction: Complied and for continuous compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit the proper and legal basis for the above-mentioned expenditures and discontinue the practice of granting these benefits, unless otherwise specifically allowed by pertinent laws and other rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with audit recommendation
All funds transferred to Barangays are already liquidated.

observations.managementAction: All unused fund transferred to LGU are already reverted to source agencies, except for funds with complete projects but there are no claims from the contractors, like the Construction of Mabbayad SWIP and Construction of NCDC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management to justify the legality and reasonableness of the expenditures and stop the grants of financial assistance and/or reimbursements incurred for private purposes, otherwise these shall be subjected to disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The management scrutinizing and monitoring that the financial assistance received by the individual or entities are for public purposes.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a. If there exists a need of the Municipality to hire consultants, i) engage only experts in their respective fields of specialization, ii) observe the rules and regulations on the hiring of consultants as provided by the above-stated law, COA and DBM issuances and iii) support payment with adequate documentation;

b. Amend the contracts of the consultants, if warranted, to effect the necessity and reasonableness of the services of consultants and to provide clearly that their services are not mere duplication of the functions of regular employees or terminate the contracts of consultants whose services are purely duplications of the functions of regular employees or whose works are already performed by existing personnel; and

c. Comply with provision of RA 9184 relative to the procurement of services of consultants.
observations.managementAction: The management already amended the contracts of the consultants cited in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (1) require the driver to submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Report of Fuel Consumption of all fuel consumption for the year
observations.managementAction: (2) designated or authorize official to sign the Driver's Trip Ticket
observations.reason: (4) establish and implement effective control/process over the use of government vehicles and fuel consumption as provided in COA Circular No. 77-61

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) establish clear-cut guidelines for the communication expenses claimed by its officials and employees; and (b) consider adopting the post-paid plans offered by the network providers for economical reasons and to foster equity in the grant thereof in relation to actual communication needs of the LGU.
observations.managementAction:
The management believed that the expenses are necessary and supported by the Appropriation Ordinance No. 2018-014.

Also, the amount is justified especially that every Department Head needed high speed of internet connection during this time of Pandemic.
observations.reason: The expenditures are still not reasonable even when compared with the highest available plans from all networks for both mobile and internet usage.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require both the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to resolve the identified variances in their respective reports and require from them the submission of reconciled records and reports in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Require both the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to prepare Annexes A to E, as assigned to each, together with proper and relevant supporting documents and submit the same to the Auditor in adherence with COA Circular No. 2015-008;
Require the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to reconcile the differences and make necessary adjustments in their respective reports; and
Enjoin the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officers the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects as required under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary reclassification of all costs to Merchandise Inventory account considering there is a clear intention of the management to sell the lots in the LGU Village per Municipal Ordinance No. 2017-012 February 20, 2017. The Municipal Accountant should also ensure that the provisions and policies under IPSAS Nos. 12 and 17 are consistently complied.

We recommended that the management should only allow payments of prior period expenses if strictly compliant with RA 7160, otherwise, the absence of appropriation especially those with budgetary requirements may cause the annual budget being inoperative in part or in its entirety.
We also recommended that the management strictly comply with the following:
Lawful expenditures and obligations incurred during a fiscal year should be taken up in the accounts of same year;
Payments of prior years’ expenses should not be charged against the current appropriation unless specifically allowed by laws, rules and regulations; and
All expenses are properly obligated and accounted in the proper accounting period.

We recommended that the Management establish the unexpended balances of LDRRMF totaling to P2,718,760.18 in the Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liability-DRRM in the Trust Fund Books.
We also recommended that the Management adhere strictly with the budgetary and funding requirements for LDRRMF as provided in RA 10121, RA 7160 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to evaluate the appropriateness of classifying the P30,600,000.00 as Continuing Appropriation, otherwise these balances should revert to the General Fund. It was also recommended that the presentation of unexpended balances of Capital Outlays and other expense class should be presented in compliance with IPSAS 24 and RA 7160.

We recommended that the management:
a) Retain ten percent (10%) from every progress payment until fifty percent (50%) of the total value works are completed as required under Sections 6.1 and 6.2 of 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184;
b) Require the contractors to post warranty security in accordance with Section 62.2.3.3 of above-stated IRR of RA 9184, which shall remain effective for one (1) year from the date of issuance of Certificate of Final Acceptance; and
c) Comply with the rules on warranties and all other related requirements provided in 2016 IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit all the prior period unremitted balances to the concerned government agencies. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant to confer with the concerned agencies to determine remedies that shall ensure that these long-outstanding balances be validated upon remittance/payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the salaries and wages of all salaried and qualified personnel of the LGU are paid in accordance with the four tranches of the Modified Salary Schedule and Salary Standardization Law or RA No. 11466, subject to compliance with the PS limitation.
In case that funds are insufficient, it was recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan formulate a modified salary schedule adopting authorized salary rates at a uniform percentage across all government positions covered by RA 11466.

We recommended that:
The Sangguniang Bayan ensure that all positions and salaries, wages, allowances and other emoluments and benefits of officials and employees which are paid wholly or mainly from the municipal funds are all incorporated in the Personnel Plantilla of the Annual Budget;
The Municipal Human Resource Management Office and Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the positions and salary grade/steps of casual employees are filled-out consistently in the Appointments and in the Approved Plantilla, in accordance with RA 11466 and CSC rules and regulations; and
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer ensure that salaries and other personnel benefits are properly accounted and paid for, respectively in accordance with laws, accounting rules and other regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Sangguniang Bayan require the creation of a separate depository account for the 20% Development Fund in compliance with Sections 310 and 313 of RA No. 7160 and Section 105, Volume I of the Manual on New Government Accounting System.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Accountable Officers to submit Acknowledgment Receipts for all donations in-kind and submit a Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and furnish copies to the Municipal Accountant and the Auditor in compliance with COA rules and regulations.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant to ensure that proper recognition and disclosure of donations in-kind are made in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to stop the grant of additional cash advances to Accountable officers until their previous cash advances are fully liquidated and supporting documents are submitted to the Municipal Accountant and to the Resident Auditor.
The Accountable Officers should be required to liquidate/settle any outstanding cash advances before closing of books to ensure that the financial transactions are properly accounted in the year. The management should adhere strictly with the COA rules on cash advances.
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require that all collections should be deposited intact daily as strictly required by the Local Treasury and Operations Manual and COA Rules.

We recommended that the expenditures be accounted in the budget and accounting reports in the appropriate financial accounts in compliance with Revised Chart of Accounts, with IPSAS and COA Circular No. 2015-003.

We recommended that the management ensure that payments for these allowances are supported with relevant and pertinent documents similarly required by COA Circular No. 2012-001 to establish compliance with the conditions set under AO No. 36 dated November 16, 2020 and reiterated under DBM – DOH Joint Circular No. 2, s. 2020 dated November 25, 2020.

We recommended that the management to submit other supporting documents that can justify and establish the reasonableness of these noted expenses for further consideration in audit.
We also reiterated our recommendation that Management (a) establish clear-cut guidelines for the communication expenses claimed by its officials and employees; and (b) consider adopting the post-paid plans offered by the network providers for economical reasons and to foster equity in the grant thereof in relation to actual communication needs of the LGU officials.
Lastly, we recommended that if cell cards/e-load are availed, consider purchasing in bulk, following the required procurement procedures and rules on withholding taxes, distribute the same in accordance with the guidelines and surrender used/consumed cellphone load cards.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require both the Supply Office and the Accounting Office to reconcile the results of the inventory count with the accounting records, effect proper adjustments in their respective records and reports and thereafter submit reconciled records and reports between both offices.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to reconcile the PPEs records.
The Management had undertaken the recommendations and submitted documents in partial compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to recognize interest expenses and applicable charges imposed on its loans upon incurrence and ensure strict compliance with the accrual basis of accounting.

We recommended that the management re-evaluate the existence of the claims and ensure that for each claim, there shall be proper and complete documents. Otherwise, those that cannot be properly established and documented shall be derecognized from the books.

We recommended that the registration and transfer of titles of the land in the name of the Municipality be prioritized by the Management in order to establish its ownership and possession and to fully protect its claims and interest over the land.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to prioritize the titling of the three (3) lots, and shall appropriate funds for processing soon.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated balances amounting to P2,599,554.54 from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund and strictly comply with the funding requirements of LDRRMF under RA No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Management computed the balances based on the available cash for the 5% DRRM Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

a) The Municipal Treasurer to continue the preparation and submission of Schedule of Unreleased Commercial Checks to Accounting Office particularly at end of the year; and

b) The Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the adjustments of the affected accounts at the end of the year and thereafter, for the ensuing year, another JV shall be drawn to reverse the previous entries made and recognize the availability of the checks for release.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned Officials to submit the lacking documents to ensure completeness, authenticity and regularity of SAP transactions and in compliance with the above-stated laws and rules. The Municipal Accountant should also ensure that the grant and liquidation of the cash advances are supported with complete and proper documents pursuant to Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The management submitted documents and updated the records in partial compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enforce collection of these receivables through the concerted efforts of both the Offices of the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor and undertake effective collection strategies and remedies as provided in RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The management admitted that significant amount of accumulated RPT and SET Receivables remained in the books of LGU and increases every year and these are inevitable. The LGU conducted tax campaigns and distributed notices every year, however, the LGU was unable to conduct the said activity in year 2020 due to the pandemic. Also, due to the crisis, some taxpayers put off in paying their taxes. Nonetheless, the Municipal Treasurer and Acting Municipal Assessor committed to undertake more effective strategies and remedies to enforce the collection of Real Property Taxes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management prioritize settling the outstanding inter-agency liabilities by remitting the overdue amounts withheld to the proper recipient government agencies in compliance with the applicable laws and rules.
observations.managementAction: The Management discussed with the Municipal Accountant and the latter settled some of the outstanding inter-agency liabilities.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a) Require concerted efforts, coordination and communication between the Accounting Office and the Human Resources Management Office (HRMO) for the proper reconciliation of the GSIS billings against the amounts to be recorded in the books;

b) Require the Human Resource Management Office to promptly update the employee records of the Municipality, submit the necessary documents such as Agency Remittance Advice (ARA) and Electronic Remittance File (ERF) and reconcile with the GSIS Office in order to ensure receipt of an updated monthly billing of employee’s premium contributions and loan amortization; and

c) Require both Offices to comply with the above-mentioned rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated with HRMO and/or GSIS. Some unremitted unmatched items and remained in the books that were later accepted by the eBCS of GSIS are for remittance. Also, the HRMO sent ARA immediately every update of member’s profile.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require that all collections should be deposited intact daily; if not, as regular as possible to avoid stocking up substantial amount of collections in the custody of the collecting officers.
observations.managementAction: The beginning balances were deposited.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the accountable persons to submit the lacking documents to ensure completeness, authenticity and regularity of SAP transactions and in compliance with the above-stated laws and rules. The Municipal Accountant should also ensure that the grant and liquidation of the cash advances are supported with complete and proper documents pursuant to Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The management submitted payrolls for consideration in audit.
The management evaluated the cash balance at year-end and presented reduction in the possible cash deficit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant impose proper withholding taxes from purchases on accounts and ensure that taxes are withheld upon occurrence of transactions and recognize the transactions in the proper liability accounts.

We recommended that the Management submit the Liquidation Reports together with the proper supporting documents and/or return these idle fund transfers to the Source Agencies, otherwise, other appropriate audit action may be enforced for the proper liquidation of these funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reviewed the Schedules of Due to NGAs, and explained that fund transfers from DOST were already implemented. Some of the DA fund transfer were implemented. Some DSWD social pensions were returned to DSWD FO2 and some were re-payroll for disbursements as of April 30, 2021. Liquidation Reports will be then prepared and submitted soon. For other funds transfers, the Municipal Accountant will continuously review the nature and sources of fund and return such fund if necessary.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management should specifically include sufficient appropriation for all statutory and contractual obligations in the Annual Budget, particularly for the LBP loans and interests which are expected to be paid yearly until 2032. In addition, the management should ensure compliance with the limitation on debt servicing as provided in RA No. 7160.

observations.managementAction: The management is exerting efforts to comply with the prescribed guidelines. The unexpended balances are based on the available cash.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management, through the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer and the Sangguniang Bayan formulate their own specific guidelines and procedures for the grant and payment of a fair, just and equitable amount of donations with full deliberation on the social and living conditions of recipients.
observations.managementAction: The Management instructed the MSWDO to draft the guidelines and will be reviewed and deliberated by the Sanguniang Bayan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:

a) Instruct the Disposal Committee to complete the documentary requirements relevant for the disposal of the unserviceable properties, undertake the disposal process and submit these to the Resident Auditor for proper action and recommendation; and

b) Require the General Services Officer and the Accounting Office to reconcile their records on all the unserviceable properties to ensure that these properties are properly accounted before disposal.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to perform the process of proper disposal and to reconcile every PPEs records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to undertake the above-procedures and file a written request for authority to write-off the noted dormant accounts in compliance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Management instructed the Municipal Accountant to review the long outstanding receivables, undertake the procedures with the coordination of concerned offices and file the request for write-off. The Municipal Accountant committed to perform the procedures however, she requested adequate time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management ensure that the Petty Cash Fund shall be utilized only for petty operating expenses and any balance at the end of the year should be liquidated within the prescribed period to ensure that transactions charged against it are accounted in the proper period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to update the List/Matrix, complete the relevant information and submit the reviewed list/matrix in order to eliminate doubts on the eligibilities of the beneficiaries. Moreover, looking forward, the updated List/Matrix shall serve to provide the Management a more accurate report on the eligibilities of families in consideration of any emergency financial assistance that may still be provided in the future.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO committed to update the List/Matrix, complete the relevant information and submit the reviewed list/matrix soon.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to: 

a) Exercise prudence in procurement of essential goods to ensure that prices of procured items are not disadvantageous to the government;

b) Safeguard public funds from economic costs and losses brought by payment of exorbitant or excessive prices inconsistent to regulated or prevailing market prices.

c) Ensure that the price freeze established by regulatory agencies is complied and applied in its procurement.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officials explained that as much as they want to follow the price freeze established by the regulatory agencies, they had no control of the prices and no other recourse than to accept the offered prices from suppliers of those essential commodities. Prices are volatile during the period of purchase, since there were demand shocks and problems with supply. Nevertheless, the concerned employees committed to comply with the price freeze set by regulatory agencies in succeeding transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to re-evaluate the appointment and/or designation of the present members of the LDRRM Office, and in case of disqualification, resort to prioritizing the pooling and recruitment of competent and eligible applicants who are qualified to fill the regular positions in the plantilla for MDRRM Office. The management should strictly observe the minimum standards and qualifications for the mandatory positions as cited on the aforementioned rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Management is re-evaluating the composition of DRRM Office, then instructed the DRRM officer to coordinate with the HRMO with all the documents needed regarding the appointments and designations and to review the organizational chart and make proper reorganization if needed. Also, the DRRMO Head was instructed to continually coordinate with the HRMO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management adopt an automated payroll system thru the government authorized depository bank which shall directly credit salaries, wages and other personnel benefits through their own bank accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Management considers the audit recommendation, however, opening of individual bank account of all Job Orders employees requires ample time, especially now that government agencies are under irregular work schedules.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor enjoin all employees and personnel to submit their DTRs and other supporting documents ready for the processing of payrolls and preparation vouchers for payment on a monthly or semi-monthly basis. The management should also ensure that services rendered of all employees and personnel are paid timely and supported with proper documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor will issue a memorandum to enjoin all employees and personnel to submit their DTRs and other supporting documents to HRMO on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate members of the Inventory Committee who shall fully devote their time and exclusively perform roles set forth in the new Guidelines. Their designation shall be effective for the period prescribed and only for the purpose of achieving a one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee prepare and submit the PIP and all the concerned offices update their records in order for the latter to strategically plan the inventory taking activities in accordance with the new guidelines.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to submit the pertinent documents required in audit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Inventory Committee and all concerned offices to:

a) Undertake the inventory taking in accordance with the procedures set in the new Guidelines; and 

b) Reconcile and update their records and reports accordingly.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to submit the pertinent documents required in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to submit basis or any exemption to the law and/or concurrence of COMELEC for further consideration in audit, otherwise the Management should comply with Sections 41, 45 and 446 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to submit the pertinent documents required in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit legal basis for the increase in the Pag-IBIG government compulsory contribution that which requires the proper consultation and approval of DBM as required under RA 11465 and RA 9679. The management should strictly observe the maximum limit of PAG-IBIG government compulsory contribution at P100.00 per month per official/employee unless the LGU consult with and secure approval of DBM.

We recommended that the Management justify the failure to withhold appropriate taxes on payments against the Cooperative which has no tax exemptions and incentives on its transactions with the LGU as a non-member; otherwise, the withholding taxes should be properly recorded in the books and thereafter be remitted to the Bureau of Internal Revenue.
observations.managementAction:
The Management already withheld taxes in the succeeding transactions with the Coop.

The Management believed that the Cooperative falls on the exemption under B) of Section 6 of Joint Rules and Regulation Implementing Articles 60, 61 and 144 of RA 9520.

Also, the management cannot withhold the amount already paid to the Coop in the year 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit the collections intact and regularly to the authorized government depository bank in compliance with the above-rules and regulations. 

Further, Management should also give priority in providing means of transportation of the Municipal Treasurer or the Cashier to the Bank especially during Fridays in order to avoid stocking up huge amount of collections on the safety vault on weekends. This will also strengthen the internal control of the Agency over the high exposure risk of cash to possible loss, misappropriation and misuse.
observations.managementAction: The management stressed that the transportation vehicles are shared in common by different department offices. As such, daily remittances by the Municipal Treasurer is not possible considering that such transportation vehicles are being used by another office for official business. The Municipality of Echague undertakes to give priority in providing means of transportation of the Municipal Treasurer or the Cashier to Bank especially during Fridays in order to avoid stocking up huge amount of collections on safety vaults on weekends. Also, the management undertakes to deposit the collections intact and regularly to the authorized government depository bank in compliance with Section 62 (C) of LTOM and Revised Cash Examination Manual.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management ensure that all its collecting officers are properly bonded in accordance with the above-stated rules, otherwise and in the absence of such, they may be held personally liable for any loss of LGU funds. Likewise, it is recommended that the renewal of the fidelity bond be applied before its expiration as required in the abovementioned regulations.
observations.managementAction: The collecting officers were already properly bonded starting in year 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Project Management Team to take immediate action on identifying qualified beneficiaries, implement the prompt roll-out of the units and ensure that the planned activities are undertaken in accordance with the Project Timeline. It is also recommended that the full possession of the twenty-two units be recovered by the Management from the unqualified LGU personnel and re-distribute these units only to qualified recipients.
observations.managementAction: The management already identified the qualified recipients and recovered units from unqualified LGU personnel.
observations.reason: There are undistributed units which remained in the LGU compound.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly abide by the procedures and guidelines set forth in COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 relative to the reporting, grant and liquidation of cash advances on Intelligence and confidential funds.

We recommended that the concerned LGU Officials and employees who are stockholders of the Cooperative to divest any interests they may have with the latter, otherwise penalties provided in the law may be enforced against erring officials. Moreover, the appointment of BAC members, Secretariat and TWG should be re-evaluated and reorganized with the assurance that no conflict of interests may arise against them.

It is also recommended that transactions with the said Cooperative be prohibited unless conflict of interests against the LGU officials is no longer apparent. 

Further, it is also recommended that the Management take necessary actions in determining if any conflicting interests may arise in its present or future dealings.
We recommended that the Management submit the above-stated lacking documents and to justify the promotion made with the 6-step increment salary of the Department Head for further evaluation in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Civil Service Commission (CSC) validation was already submitted
observations.reason: The submitted documents are subjected to further review on retroactive approval of Budget for the salaries.
