observations.managementAction:
Some procured items were not presented for inspection to COA, since most purchases during the year 2020 were used/consumed immediately by the end-users.

The management is exerting efforts to present for inspection all procured items.
observations.reason: Procured items were not presented for inspection.

observations.managementAction:
The management used the QRF only during the declaration of state of calamity.

DRRM funds for the year 2020 were almost exhausted and used in response to the pandemic.

The disbursements of fund for the year 2020 are all within the LDRRM Plan, AIP or revised plans,

RAAO for QRF, MOOE and CO were already maintained and prepared by the Budget Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management should strictly observe the provisions of DBM-DOH Joint Circular No.1, s. 2012 as amended by DBM-DOH Joint Circular No.1 s. 2016 in the payment of hazard, subsistence and laundry allowances of the Public Health Workers and provide for sufficient appropriation for said allowances as authorized under the law and rules.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to a) direct the concerned Officials to update their records and documents which pertains to the receipt, issuance and disposal of PPE and reconciliation between the Accounting and Supply Office be undertaken to address the noted differences in their reports; and ensure that valuation and disclosure of Property, Plant and Equipment accounts be made in compliance with PPSAS.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer shall reconcile the noted inconsistencies in the books and in the physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment.
The management commented that it shall undertake to prepare the Local Road Inventory and Road Map through the assistance of the barangays.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:

a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the transactions to the proper asset accounts and the Supply Officer to establish accountability over these assets; and

b) Ensure compliance with IPSAS and applicable accounting rules and regulations on the recording and classification of financial transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was directed to reclassify the accounts erroneously recorded as Other MOOE instead of Capital Outlay.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

a) Address and justify the noted inconsistencies in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund out of the Current Year Appropriation and Continuing Appropriations;

b) Stop the charging of expenditures out of the Fund which are not allowed by the reviewing authorities, the law and other rules; and

c) Ensure that the 20% Development Fund is appropriated and utilized in accordance with the law, rules and regulations, otherwise the transactions shall be suspended or disallowed, as the case may be.
observations.managementAction: The Management commented that they shall submit the supporting documents to justify the transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit the proper and legal basis for the above-mentioned expenditures and discontinue the practice of granting these benefits, unless otherwise specifically allowed by pertinent laws and other rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The management shall submit all the necessary documents in support to the Rewards and Incentives of the Municipal Employees.
The Municipal Accountant was directed to send demand letters to all the concerned barangays to immediately liquidate the assistance to the barangays given or return the funds to the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the unexpended balances be returned to the concerned Source Agencies and/or remit the same immediately to the National Treasury, as required by the afore-cited rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Management has been gathering records relative to the status of implementation of these projects. As compared with last year’s finding, the P16,368,001.64 balance has already been reduced to P4,993,971.97 this year. The Management is exhausting all possible remedies to identify whether these remaining balances need to be returned or can be used by the LGU for other purposes if the same is already fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management to justify the legality and reasonableness of the expenditures and stop the grants of financial assistance and/or reimbursements incurred for private purposes, otherwise these shall be subjected to disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The Management commented that while the financial assistance to private individuals, the same was given for public purpose in support to the programs and projects of the LGU. Assistance was given mostly to indigent individuals, to women organization in support to women in the municipality, and to private organizations whose programs are in line with the programs and projects of the LGU.

The Municipal Accountant was directed to require concerned LGU employees and officials to submit all the necessary documents in support to their travelling expenses be made only upon presentation of complete supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management abide by the standards and rules set forth in RA 9184 as well as COA Circulars and commit to improve its procurement undertakings consistent with the law, otherwise those identified procurement transactions which still lack supporting documents to support its legality, propriety and legality shall be subjected to suspension or disallowance as the case may be.

observations.managementAction: The management committed to undertake to submit through the MPDO, MAO and MBO the Annual Accomplishment Report for the proper monitoring and evaluation of the GAD related activities and other supporting documents to justify the utilization of the approved GAD funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

a) The Local Chief Executive shall direct the Municipal Assessor to submit copy of the Assessment Roll on or before the 31st day of December of each year and coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for proper reconciliation of their records to come up with a complete and accurate list showing the name of the taxpayers with the amounts due and collectible as basis in setting up the correct Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables from taxpayers which is necessary in order to fairly present the RPT/SET Receivables in the books of the Municipality.

b) The Municipal Accountant should set up RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s Index Cards supported by a duly certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year.

We recommended that the Management: a) exert efforts in exhausting all possible remedies to collect the long-outstanding and non-moving receivables; b) determine existence of any impairment of the receivables and recognize allowance for impairment in the books; c) In case of existence of applicable conditions set forth under Section 7.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-005, submit a written request for write-off to be supported by the required documents specified in Section 8.3 of the same Circular.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a. If there exists a need of the Municipality to hire consultants, i) engage only experts in their respective fields of specialization, ii) observe the rules and regulations on the hiring of consultants as provided by the above-stated law, COA and DBM issuances and iii) support payment with adequate documentation;

b. Amend the contracts of the consultants, if warranted, to effect the necessity and reasonableness of the services of consultants and to provide clearly that their services are not mere duplication of the functions of regular employees or terminate the contracts of consultants whose services are purely duplications of the functions of regular employees or whose works are already performed by existing personnel; and

c. Comply with provision of RA 9184 relative to the procurement of services of consultants.
observations.managementAction:
Consultants of the LGU were primarily hired with a function as adviser on matters relative to their expertise. Some were hired on the basis of their proven experience and capability which ordinary employees do not possess. 

We undertake to amend the contracts of those whose contacts do specifically state that their functions are not a mere duplication of the functions of regular employees because while they may have not been expressly stated in their contract, their functions are not a duplication of any function being performed by a regular employee.

We also undertake to comply with the provisions of RA 9184 relative to the hiring of consultants.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local School Board, through the Municipal Mayor as Chairman, include in the SEF Budget programs/projects/activities specifically authorized under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 and implement the same in order to maximize the use of the SEF for its intended purpose. 

Likewise, the Local School Board shall have a conscious and concerted effort of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute.

We recommended that the management exert efforts in reconciling the noted-variances and inconsistencies in the submitted reports, ascertain existence of all the recorded properties, plant and equipment and conduct an appraisal of the unserviceable properties and initiate its disposal.
observations.managementAction: A memorandum has been issued to the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to reconcile inconsistencies in the books and in the physical count of Property Plant and Equipment and identify unserviceable properties subject for disposal through the Appraisal and Disposal Committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (1) require the driver to submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Report of Fuel Consumption of all fuel consumption for the year; (2) designated or authorize official to sign the Driver's Trip Ticket; (3) ensure that all supporting document a are properly accomplished and transmitted for audit and; (4) establish and implement effective control/process over the use of government vehicles and fuel consumption as provided in COA Circular No. 77-61
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the Report of Fuel Consumption.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 75-6 and ensure that all vehicles and equipment be marked with "For Official Use Only" followed by the name of the Municipality of Echague to safeguard government property against potential mismanagement and misuse.
observations.managementAction: We are on the process of placing the marks “For Official Use Only” on the remaining service vehicles not yet marked.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management cause the disposal of the unserviceable equipment in accordance with existing rules and regulations in order to protect them from further deterioration and to generate additional funds for the Municipality. Updating of its inventory is necessary prior to the conduct of the disposal of these PPE for proper identification of the unserviceable items for inclusion during the disposal.
observations.managementAction: We are preparing the documents and will request for the disposal of the unserviceable service vehicles.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management require the Inventory Committee to complete their inventory and submit the required Report of Physical Count of Inventory of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) at least once a year. The Property and Supply Management Division should maintain property ledger cards for each class of equipment. Reconciliation is likewise to be made by the concerned offices with the equipment ledger cards, with the balances of general ledger and the subsidiary ledgers to provide the assurance that the account balances are fairly presented in the Financial Statement.
observations.managementAction: A memorandum has been issued by the Local Chief Executive to the Municipal GSO to conduct inventory and maintain property ledger cards for each class of equipment as required under the Manual of NGAs for LGUs.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management require the Inventory Committee to complete their inventory and submit the required Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RCPPE) at least once a year. The Property and Supply Management Division should maintain property ledger cards for each class of equipment. Also, the RCPPE should be reconciled with the equipment ledger cards and with the balances in the general ledger and the subsidiary ledgers to provide the assurance that the account balances are fairly presented in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: A memorandum has been issued by the Local Chief Executive to the Municipal GSO to conduct inventory and maintain property ledger cards for each class of equipment as required under the Manual of NGAs for LGUs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) establish clear-cut guidelines for the communication expenses claimed by its officials and employees; and (b) consider adopting the post-paid plans offered by the network providers for economical reasons and to foster equity in the grant thereof in relation to actual communication needs of the LGU.
observations.managementAction:
The management believed that the expenses are necessary and supported by the Appropriation Ordinance No. 2018-014.

Also, the management cannot withhold the amount already paid to the Coop in the year 2020. | Partially Implemented.

The submitted documents are subjected to further review on retroactive approval of Budget for the salaries.
observations.reason: The expenditures are still not reasonable even when compared with the highest available plans from all networks for both mobile and internet usage.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require both the Supply Office and the Accounting Office to reconcile the results of the inventory count with the accounting records, effect proper adjustments in their respective records and reports and thereafter submit reconciled records and reports between both offices.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to recognize interest expenses and applicable charges imposed on its loans upon incurrence and ensure strict compliance with the accrual basis of accounting.

We recommended that the management re-evaluate the existence of the claims and ensure that for each claim, there shall be proper and complete documents. Otherwise, those that cannot be properly established and documented shall be derecognized from the books.

We recommended that the registration and transfer of titles of the land in the name of the Municipality be prioritized by the Management in order to establish its ownership and possession and to fully protect its claims and interest over the land.

We recommended the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated balances amounting to P2,599,554.54 from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund and strictly comply with the funding requirements of LDRRMF under RA No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
The Municipal Treasurer to continue the preparation and submission of Schedule of Unreleased Commercial Checks to Accounting Office particularly at end of the year; and
The Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the adjustments of the affected accounts at the end of the year and thereafter, for the ensuing year, another JV shall be drawn to reverse the previous entries made and recognize the availability of the checks for release.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned Officials to submit the lacking documents to ensure completeness, authenticity and regularity of SAP transactions and in compliance with the above-stated laws and rules. The Municipal Accountant should also ensure that the grant and liquidation of the cash advances are supported with complete and proper documents pursuant to Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enforce collection of these receivables through the concerted efforts of both the Offices of the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor and undertake effective collection strategies and remedies as provided in RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the management prioritize settling the outstanding inter-agency liabilities by remitting the overdue amounts withheld to the proper recipient government agencies in compliance with the applicable laws and rules.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Require concerted efforts, coordination and communication between the Accounting Office and the Human Resources Management Office (HRMO) for the proper reconciliation of the GSIS billings against the amounts to be recorded in the books;
Require the Human Resource Management Office to promptly update the employee records of the Municipality, submit the necessary documents such as Agency Remittance Advice (ARA) and Electronic Remittance File (ERF) and reconcile with the GSIS Office in order to ensure receipt of an updated monthly billing of employee’s premium contributions and loan amortization; and
Require both Offices to comply with the above-mentioned rules and regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require that all collections should be deposited intact daily; if not, as regular as possible to avoid stocking up substantial amount of collections in the custody of the collecting officers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the accountable persons to submit proper and complete documents that shall show and establish expenditures related to COVID-19 PPAs, otherwise audit suspension as may be proper.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant impose proper withholding taxes from purchases on accounts and ensure that taxes are withheld upon occurrence of transactions and recognize the transactions in the proper liability accounts.

We recommended that the Management submit the Liquidation Reports together with the proper supporting documents and/or return these idle fund transfers to the Source Agencies, otherwise, other appropriate audit action may be enforced for the proper liquidation of these funds.

We recommended that the Management should specifically include sufficient appropriation for all statutory and contractual obligations in the Annual Budget, particularly for the LBP loans and interests which are expected to be paid yearly until 2032. In addition, the management should ensure compliance with the limitation on debt servicing as provided in RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Management, through the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer and the Sangguniang Bayan formulate their own specific guidelines and procedures for the grant and payment of a fair, just and equitable amount of donations with full deliberation on the social and living conditions of recipients.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
Instruct the Disposal Committee to complete the documentary requirements relevant for the disposal of the unserviceable properties, undertake the disposal process and submit these to the Resident Auditor for proper action and recommendation; and
Require the General Services Officer and the Accounting Office to reconcile their records on all the unserviceable properties to ensure that these properties are properly accounted before disposal.

We recommended the Management to undertake the above-procedures and file a written request for authority to write-off the noted dormant accounts in compliance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Management ensure that the Petty Cash Fund shall be utilized only for petty operating expenses and any balance at the end of the year should be liquidated within the prescribed period to ensure that transactions charged against it are accounted in the proper period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to update the List/Matrix, complete the relevant information and submit the reviewed list/matrix in order to eliminate doubts on the eligibilities of the beneficiaries. Moreover, looking forward, the updated List/Matrix shall serve to provide the Management a more accurate report on the eligibilities of families in consideration of any emergency financial assistance that may still be provided in the future.

We recommended Management to:
Exercise prudence in procurement of essential goods to ensure that prices of procured items are not disadvantageous to the government;
Safeguard public funds from economic costs and losses brought by payment of exorbitant or excessive prices inconsistent to regulated or prevailing market prices.
Ensure that the price freeze established by regulatory agencies is complied and applied in its procurement.

We recommended Management to re-evaluate the appointment and/or designation of the present members of the LDRRM Office, and in case of disqualification, resort to prioritizing the pooling and recruitment of competent and eligible applicants who are qualified to fill the regular positions in the plantilla for MDRRM Office. The management should strictly observe the minimum standards and qualifications for the mandatory positions as cited on the aforementioned rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Management adopt an automated payroll system thru the government authorized depository bank which shall directly credit salaries, wages and other personnel benefits through their own bank accounts.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor enjoin all employees and personnel to submit their DTRs and other supporting documents ready for the processing of payrolls and preparation vouchers for payment on a monthly or semi-monthly basis. The management should also ensure that services rendered of all employees and personnel are paid timely and supported with proper documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate members of the Inventory Committee who shall fully devote their time and exclusively perform roles set forth in the new Guidelines. Their designation shall be effective for the period prescribed and only for the purpose of achieving a one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee prepare and submit the PIP and all the concerned offices update their records in order for the latter to strategically plan the inventory taking activities in accordance with the new guidelines.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Inventory Committee and all concerned offices to:
Undertake the inventory taking in accordance with the procedures set in the new Guidelines; and
Reconcile and update their records and reports accordingly.

We recommended the Management to submit basis or any exemption to the law and/or concurrence of COMELEC for further consideration in audit, otherwise the Management should comply with Sections 41, 45 and 446 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Management submit legal basis for the increase in the Pag-IBIG government compulsory contribution that which requires the proper consultation and approval of DBM as required under RA 11465 and RA 9679. The management should strictly observe the maximum limit of PAG-IBIG government compulsory contribution at P100.00 per month per official/employee unless the LGU consult with and secure approval of DBM.

We recommended that the Management justify the failure to withhold appropriate taxes on payments against the Cooperative which has no tax exemptions and incentives on its transactions with the LGU as a non-member; otherwise, the withholding taxes should be properly recorded in the books and thereafter be remitted to the Bureau of Internal Revenue.
observations.managementAction:
The Management already withheld taxes in the succeeding transactions with the Coop.

The Management believed that the Cooperative falls on the exemption under B) of Section 6 of Joint Rules and Regulation Implementing Articles 60, 61 and 144 of RA 9520.

Also, the management cannot withhold the amount already paid to the Coop in the year 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit the collections intact and regularly to the authorized government depository bank in compliance with the above-rules and regulations. 

Further, Management should also give priority in providing means of transportation of the Municipal Treasurer or the Cashier to the Bank especially during Fridays in order to avoid stocking up huge amount of collections on the safety vault on weekends. This will also strengthen the internal control of the Agency over the high exposure risk of cash to possible loss, misappropriation and misuse.
observations.managementAction: The management stressed that the transportation vehicles are shared in common by different department offices. As such, daily remittances by the Municipal Treasurer is not possible considering that such transportation vehicles are being used by another office for official business. The Municipality of Echague undertakes to give priority in providing means of transportation of the Municipal Treasurer or the Cashier to Bank especially during Fridays in order to avoid stocking up huge amount of collections on safety vaults on weekends. Also, the management undertakes to deposit the collections intact and regularly to the authorized government depository bank in compliance with Section 62 (C) of LTOM and Revised Cash Examination Manual.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management ensure that all its collecting officers are properly bonded in accordance with the above-stated rules, otherwise and in the absence of such, they may be held personally liable for any loss of LGU funds. Likewise, it is recommended that the renewal of the fidelity bond be applied before its expiration as required in the abovementioned regulations.
observations.managementAction: The collecting officers were already properly bonded starting in year 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the fidelity bond of the accountable officers should be increased accordingly in compliance to the above-mentioned rules and regulations in order to protect government funds.
observations.managementAction: The fidelity bond of nine (9) accountable officers were already increased in year 2020, sufficient to cover their respective accountabilities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all the Accountable Officers prepare and submit the required documents and reports and ensure that the Certification are made in the Cashbooks in accordance with the above-rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The accountable officers already prepared cashbooks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Project Management Team to take immediate action on identifying qualified recipients/beneficiaries, implement the prompt roll-out of the units and ensure that the planned activities are undertaken in accordance with the Project Timeline. It is also recommended that the full possession of the twenty-two units be recovered by the Management from the unqualified LGU personnel and re-distribute these units only to qualified recipients.
observations.managementAction: The management already identified the qualified recipients and recovered units from unqualified LGU personnel.
observations.reason: There are undistributed units which remained in the LGU compound.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management ensure that its revenue-generating activities will have a potential impact on its overall operations, particularly on its net assets/equity and that which will provide a more realizable and concrete future economic benefit or service potential to the Agency.
observations.managementAction: The management now operated its owned business – LGU Hardware and gasoline station and realized net income of P128,332.65 during the year 2020.

observations.recommendation: We commended the Management on their effective implementation of the above-cited minimum standards on disaster preparedness and we recommended that they continue such good practices to strengthen further their capacities as well as of the communities to anticipate, cope and recover from the negative impacts of emergency occurrences and disasters.
observations.managementAction: The management is taking efforts to continue good practices.

The LGU has unexpended balance/continuing appropriation for loan repayments in year 2020, so the Municipal Budget Officer appropriated only the balance in the Annual Budget the repayments for 2021.

Loan repayments were incorporated in Annual Budget for year 2021 and the year 2020 unexpended continuing appropriation.
observations.reason: Similar observations reiterated in CY 2020 audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly abide by the procedures and guidelines set forth in COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 relative to the reporting, grant and liquidation of cash advances on Intelligence and confidential funds.
observations.managementAction: The LGU followed the procedures and guidelines with regard to reporting, grant and utilization of cash advance of confidential funds.
observations.reason: Continuous monitoring until COA-Central Office (CO) confirms results of their audit of expenditures.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the concerned LGU Officials and employees who are stockholders of the Cooperative to divest any interests they may have with the latter, otherwise penalties provided in the law may be enforced against erring officials. Moreover, the appointment of BAC members, Secretariat and TWG should be re-evaluated and reorganized with the assurance that no conflict of interests may arise against them.

It is also recommended that transactions with the said Cooperative be prohibited unless conflict of interests against the LGU officials is no longer apparent. 

Further, it is also recommended that the Management take necessary actions in determining if any conflicting interests may arise in its present or future dealings.
observations.managementAction: Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) members who are stakeholders to the said Cooperative already divested their shares/interests.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit the above-stated lacking documents and to justify the promotion made with the 6-step increment salary of the Department Head for further evaluation in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Civil Service Commission (CSC) validation was already submitted
observations.reason: The submitted documents are subjected to further review on retroactive approval of Budget for the salaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to endeavor in improving their cash management, reassess their planning, budget execution and monitoring processes and assertively pursue feasible solutions that will address the issue involving fiscal operations of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The finance team prepared and followed the Cash Program to monitor the cash flows and limit the release of allotment up to available cash and future collections. 

Unexpected expenditures were incurred during the COVID pandemic, despite the efforts of the LGU to minimize spending.

The Treasury Office is still verifying the discrepancies found by the accounting office.

a) The accounting office already performing the cash reconciliation of accounting and treasury books, most errors/discrepancies were already adjusted.

b) The treasury office now regularly requests the snap shot copies from bank.

c) Most reconciling items were already adjusted, except for some debit/credit memos and unsupported disbursements, both just in minimal amount. The management will inquire with the LBP Branch.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management should:

a) Avoid appropriations for projects which do not partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures and which are not specifically allowable under the said JMC;

b) Ensure compliance with the conditions and review comments cited by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan; and

c) Adhere with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1, RA 7160 and other applicable laws and rules.
observations.managementAction: The 20% EDF was already appropriated for capital outlay except when such funds were allowed to be used for COVID related PPAs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

a) Ensure that 20% DF are appropriated for capital outlays and avoid appropriations which do not partake the nature of investments or capital expenditures;

b) Commit the balances of the 2019 20% DF and the Continuing Appropriations only to projects allowed by the JMC and as consistent with Section 322 of RA 7160; and

c) Adhere to the above laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The 20% EDF was already appropriated for capital outlay except when such funds were allowed to be used for COVID related PPAs.
observations.reason: The management failed to submit Sangguniang Panlalawigan (SP) review comments for evaluation of compliance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

a) Stop charging ineligible expenses against the 20% Development Fund; and

b) Ensure that the appropriation of project expenditures and/or utilization of Funds are strictly in accordance with the JMC.
observations.managementAction: The 20% EDF was already appropriated for capital outlay except when such funds were allowed to be used for COVID related PPAs.
observations.reason: The management failed to submit SP review comments for evaluation of compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management should adopt the proper accounting treatment of borrowing costs and ensure full disclosure of pertinent information be made in the Financial Statements consistent with IPSAS 5.
observations.managementAction: Interest on loan incurred during the construction of projects were already adjusted and added to the project cost.

The conduct of physical count of road network was intermittently performed due to the unusual work schedule of employees during this time of pandemic.

The Inventory Committee are still conducting the physical count and the GSO is still preparing the report.

All other recommendations were complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to 1) show for verification in audit the land titles as proof of ownership of the parcels of land, otherwise, the LGU’s rights and ownership over the lots are doubtful and may be exposed to future adverse claims; 2) provide for details and other more reliable documents supporting the increase in the land account and 3) ensure that legal rights and possession over the lots are fully established before recognition in the books of the LGU.
observations.managementAction:
On-going processing of required documents for titling of land.

Processing of lot title requires ample time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly recognize the effects of financial transactions into the appropriate financial accounts in order to achieve a fair presentation, understandability and reliability of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already used the proper account for sales of inventories into Sales Revenue account and cost of sold items into Cost of Sales account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize in the proper period the Receivable from the Disallowance in compliance with COA Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts.
observations.managementAction: The said receivables were already recognized in the books of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit the above-listed documents for all the projects that were implemented in CY 2019 and those which construction or implementation are still on-going in order that an objective evaluation of the contracts and proper inspection be undertaken.
observations.managementAction:
The management already attached the required documents in the succeeding disbursements related to construction projects.

The Engineering Office is preparing the lacking documents for submission to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management ensure that deliveries of procured goods be inspected and validated by the audit team in compliance with the laws, rules and regulations. It also recommended that the resident auditor be notified within 24 hours from acceptance, the time, day and place of delivery to allow the auditor to confirm the conduct of inspection and validation.
observations.managementAction:
Some procured items were not presented for inspection to COA, since most purchases during the year 2020 were used/consumed immediately by the end-users.

The management is exerting efforts to present for inspection all procured items.

The 2019 continuing appropriation for LDRRM was transferred to Trust Fund and not maintained in the General Fund since the management had the plan to re-program the fund into MOOE, to be used during the year 2020 for COVID 19 mitigation PPAs.

observations.reason: With similar observations reiterated in CY 2020 audit

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management should strictly observe the provisions of DBM-DOH Joint Circular No.1, s. 2012 as amended by DBM-DOH Joint Circular No.1 s. 2016 in the payment of hazard, subsistence and laundry allowances of the Public Health Workers and provide for sufficient appropriation for said allowances as authorized under the law and rules.
observations.managementAction: The management adjusted already the said rates of RHU personnel starting November 2020.

The management explained that the donations from Rio Norte are not subject to any condition, hence the same can be recognized as an outright revenue. The LGU Village reservation fees and New Public Market Occupancy fees are non-refundable; hence if prospective buyer/lessees will not proceed with the sale/lease, the same can be recognized as outright revenues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to a) direct the concerned Officials to update their records and documents which pertains to the receipt, issuance and disposal of PPE and reconciliation between the Accounting and Supply Office be undertaken to address the noted differences in their reports; and ensure that valuation and disclosure of Property, Plant and Equipment accounts be made in compliance with PPSAS.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer shall reconcile the noted inconsistencies in the books and in the physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment.

The management committed to exhaust all possible remedies to remit the prior year unremitted balances. The Municipal Accountant was directed to inquire from all the government agencies where these unremitted balances are due to determine proper remedies on these amount withheld.
The management commented that it shall undertake to prepare the Local Road Inventory and Road Map through the assistance of the barangays.

observations.recommendation:

Sometime in November 13, 2017, the Sanggunian Bayan declared the Municipality under the State of Calamity. The effects of calamities started to affect individuals when Typhoon Jolina hit Northern Luzon. The damaged caused by Typhoon Jolina was aggravated when just after typhoon, continuous heavy rain caused severe damage to agricultural crops not to mention damaged caused to properties especially to residents in the low lying areas.

While it may be true that the Sangguniang Bayan declared calamity, which is the continuous heavy rain, still exists at the time it was declared. The reason for declaration was not merely due to Typhoon Jolina. The damaged caused by Typhoon Jolina was strongly felt in November because it was aggravated by the continuous heavy rains which severely damaged crops and properties which prompted us to declare the municipality under the State of Calamity on November to alleviate the sufferings of those affected by flooding.

The recipients were all disaster stricken individuals whose crops, houses and other properties were severely damaged due to Typhoon Jolina and heavy rains. They may have been categorized based on their work but it is for purpose of prompt distribution of the assistance which is all disaster stricken individuals. Request for assistance from the different barangays and certification from the Municipal Agriculture Office will be submitted for your reference.

Considering the need to provide immediate assistance to disaster stricken individuals, we are left with no other option but to distribute the same in the form of cash as authorized by the Sangguniang Bayan through Appropriation Ordinance No. 2017-013 authorizing the distribution thereof. The same has been distributed in the form of cash in order to facilitate the immediate distribution thereof due to the unavailability of stores who could immediately provide the required quantity of goods. Due to lack of supply, we are left with no other option but to distribute cash to the victims.

b.Medicines procured were not used for sale. The stated purpose that it will be for the Botika ng Bayan is merely a clerical error as reflected in the Journal Voucher.

It cannot be denied that there are legal issues which require immediate action on the part of the LGU. Considering that the Municipality of Echague has no Municipal Legal Officer of its own, referring the said issue to the Provincial Legal Officer and Provincial Prosecutor takes time due to the number of cases these agencies are handling due to the continuous trial setting of the courts. In order to address this problem, the LGU hired two (2) consultants who can immediately render legal service to the LGU.

While it may have been written in their contract of service that they will act as Legal Counsel of the Local Chief Executive, it does not mean that they immediately acted as such. It was only written because when the need arises as when the Provincial Legal Office cannot represent the LGU, the two consultants undertake to act as Legal Counsel after complying with the requirements as provided for under the Local Government Code and other pertinent law. No courts or agency will allow any private lawyers to represent an LGU in any case without the submission of proper documents. Moreover, the hiring of private lawyers by local government units are governed by the Local Government Code. Under COA Circular No. 98-002, in the letter of Silvestre Bello III, acting then as the Solicitor General, clarified the issue that the OSG is not the legal counsel of the LGUs, not being their lawyer, the OSG cannot also deputize private lawyers to handle their cases. With this issuance, it resulted to the amendment of circulars insofar as the LGUs are concerned, to wit: “In view thereof, the last paragraph of COA Circular Nos. 95—011 and 86-255, dated December 4, 1995 and April 2, 1986, respectively are hereby amended insofar as LGUs are concerned, to read as follows: “xxx xxxxxx. In the event that such legal services cannot be avoided or is justified under extraordinary or exceptional circumstances for government agencies and instrumentalities, including GOCC, the written conformity and acquiescence of the Solicitor General or the Government Corporate Counsel, as the case maybe, and the written concurrence of the Commission on Audit shall first be secured before hiring or employment of private law firm. With respect to the local government units, only in those instances provided in par. 3(1) Section of 481 of RA 7160 may public funds be utilized as payment for the services of a private legal counsel.” It must be emphasized that when the said circulars were amended, in requiring the conformity of the OSG, it did not include in the enumeration the LGUs. Under the statutory construction, when the law does not include, it excludes. The circular is very clear in excluding the LGUs in the agencies which requires the concurrence of OSG before hiring a private lawyer, otherwise, if such is not the intention, it would have included LGU in the enumeration or expressly stated the need for OSG concurrence for LGUs. Such has been the ruling because clearly, the hiring of private lawyers by LGUs is now governed by the Local Government Code of 1991 which did not mention to secure the OSG conformity. In addition, the lawyers rendered services. To require the return of the honoraria paid to them would result to unjust enrichment as the services has already been rendered. However, from today, we undertake to terminate the services of the two (2) legal consultants as legal counsel.

CY 2017 AAR | Sometime in November 13, 2017, the Sanggunian Bayan declared the Municipality under the State of Calamity. The effects of calamities started to affect individuals when Typhoon Jolina hit Northern Luzon. The damaged caused by Typhoon Jolina was aggravated when just after typhoon, continuous heavy rain caused severe damage to agricultural crops not to mention damaged caused to properties especially to residents in the low lying areas.

While it may be true that the Sangguniang Bayan declared calamity, which is the continuous heavy rain, still exists at the time it was declared. The reason for declaration was not merely due to Typhoon Jolina. The damaged caused by Typhoon Jolina was strongly felt in November because it was aggravated by the continuous heavy rains which severely damaged crops and properties which prompted us to declare the municipality under the State of Calamity on November to alleviate the sufferings of those affected by flooding.

The recipients were all disaster stricken individuals whose crops, houses and other properties were severely damaged due to Typhoon Jolina and heavy rains. They may have been categorized based on their work but it is for purpose of prompt distribution of the assistance which is all disaster stricken individuals. Request for assistance from the different barangays and certification from the Municipal Agriculture Office will be submitted for your reference.

Considering the need to provide immediate assistance to disaster stricken individuals, we are left with no other option but to distribute the same in the form of cash as authorized by the Sangguniang Bayan through Appropriation Ordinance No. 2017-013 authorizing the distribution thereof. The same has been distributed in the form of cash in order to facilitate the immediate distribution thereof due to the unavailability of stores who could immediately provide the required quantity of goods. Due to lack of supply, we are left with no other option but to distribute cash to the victims.

b.Medicines procured were not used for sale. The stated purpose that it will be for the Botika ng Bayan is merely a clerical error as reflected in the Journal Vouches.

It bears emphasizing that as gleaned from the Disbursement Voucher, indicated purpose is for the MDRRMO to be used for individuals affected by disaster/calamities. The purpose stated in the Journal Vouches was merely erroneously indicated as for Botika ng Bayan because the correct purpose is for the MDRRMO as stated in the journal voucher.

c. We undertake to utilize the LDRRM Fund under Disaster Prevention and Mitigations and Risk Reduction and Climate Change Adaptation as appropriated.

While the LDRRM Fund Investment Plan of the LGU does not previously present the activities to be funded out of the Special Trust Fund, however, the same has been revised and was included therein the revised plan for the utilization of the Special Trust Fund from the unexpended balances of the previous years. In addition, QRF from the previous years will be submitted by the MDRRMO. 

Moreover, on the observation of the procurement of K-9 dogs, while the Supplier is not registered as dog supplier, the said transaction is advantageous to the government as the supplier is a known Trainor of Dogs and the exclusive dealer of K-9 dogs in the province by an international distributor, hence, we were assured that the K-9 dogs delivered to us were capable to perform the purpose for which it was purchased because the same has been delivered by a Trainor competent in the field of training K-9 dogs. A copy of the certification as to that effect will be submitted by the MDRRMO.

CY 2017 AAR | The recommendation is noted. We undertake to utilize Gender and Development Budget through programs and activities which will address gender issues within the LGU and ensure the implementation of Magna Carta of Women and other relevant issuances.

Moreover, last year, the Municipality conceptualized livelihood project for women in the LGU, however, it did not materialize on time due to the delay in the posting of necessary documents for its implementation which resulted to the utilization of only five (5%) of the funds. This year this project will be implemented using the Gender and Development Fund as appropriated. 

Additional report on utilization of GAD funds will be submitted by the Office of the MPDC.

CY 2017 AAR | We undertake to utilize the LCPC Fund as appropriated through the conduct of more programs and projects for the protection of the children and promotion of their rights and welfare.

The Municipality is actually conducting yearly activities, among of which is Day of Youth for the promotion of the rights and welfare of children during the month of December in coordination with the National Youth Commission.

Moreover, the partial utilization of the LCPC fund is due to the fact that last year, we had a meeting with the PGI concerning the creation of project UBING. The said project aims to establish quick reaction unit to victims of child abuses. In the said project, there will be a Memorandum of Agreement to be executed by and between the PGI and other LGUs  in the province for the setting of counterparts of the LGUs for the project. Hence, considering that the said project is for the protection and welfare of the children, we committed to cooperate with the PGI and waited for the implementation materialize last year.

Additional report on utilization of the project but which did not 
will be submitted by the Office of MPDC.

CY 2017 AAR | We undertake to conduct activities in coordination with the Office of the Senior Citizen Affairs (OSCA) to give support to the improvement of their well-being and at the same time addressing the concerns of senior citizens and persons with disabilities.
